title: Fix m28-profiles-cache.test.js — cache timestamp not updated after fetch (regression)
description:
STILL FAILING — 1 test in test/m28-profiles-cache.test.js:

FAIL: 'updates cache timestamp after successful fetch'
AssertionError: expected 1775158639329 to be greater than or equal to 1775849839330

The test creates an expired cache (>7 days old), calls getProfile(), and expects the cache file to be rewritten with a fresh timestamp. But the timestamp in the written file is still the old value (1775158639329), not a new Date.now().

This means profiles.js IS fetching (test 1 passes now) but is NOT updating the timestamp when writing the refreshed cache. Check the cache write path in profiles.js — after a successful remote fetch, the written JSON must include timestamp: Date.now(), not the old cached timestamp.

Previous task (task-1775847933739) was marked done but this test still fails.
